Week 8 Topic
DML Transactions/Lookups
Use Cases

Page 8
Autocommit
Default mode for most database systems
UPDATE Staff SET gender ='F';
Query OK, 45364 rows affected (0.05 sec)
All changes are
immediately
permanent.
UPDATE Staff SET gender ='F'
WHERE staffID=1234;
AAAGH
Should have been

Page 9
Turn off Autocommit
SET AUTOCOMMIT = FALSE;
UPDATE Staff SET gender ='F';
Query OK, 45364 rows affected (0.15 sec)
ROLLBACK;
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.06 sec)
UPDATE Staff SET gender ='F'
WHERE staffID=1234;
Query OK, 1 row affected (0.06 sec)
COMMIT;
Oops
bugger

Page 10
Autocommit and Transactions
› Transaction
INSERT INTO supply (prod_id, quantity, arrival)
VALUES ('LW24453', 3000, '2015-09-26';
UPDATE stocklevel
SET quantity = quantity + 3000
WHERE prod_id = 'LW24453';
COMMIT;
Not permanent yet. Will
disappear if there is a
problem
Not permanent. Will be
undone if there is a
problem
Both changes are
permanent now.

Page 11
Transactions in MySQL suspend Autocommit
START TRANSACTION
INSERT INTO supply (prod_id, quantity, arrival)
VALUES ('LW24453', 3000, '2015-09-26';
UPDATE stocklevel
SET quantity = quantity + 3000
WHERE prod_id = 'LW24453';
COMMIT;
Not permanent yet. Will
disappear if there is a
problem
Not permanent. Will be
undone if there is a
problem
Both changes are
permanent now.

Page 13
Predicate Comparison Operators
…WHERE startDate BETWEEN '2015-10-01' AND '2015-10-31';
…WHERE state (NOT) IN ('WA', 'VIC', 'NSW');
…WHERE salary >= 1000 AND age <= 50;
…WHERE state IS NOT NULL;
…WHERE state IS NULL;
…WHERE salary >= 1000 OR position = 'manager';
…WHERE name LIKE 'A%';
WHERE name LIKE 'A_%';

Page 14
Projection, Aliases and Concatenation
SELECT category || ' - ' || name AS description, location AS shelf
FROM Product;
description
shelf
